old_value: {"id":"TGR-l8h","title":"Fix √g coefficient bug in perturbation crosscheck","description":"In examples/15_perturbation_spectrum_crosscheck.jl line 49, the coefficient 1//8 in the (tr h)² term of sqrt_g_correction() should be 1//4. This is derived from δ²(√g·L)/√g₀ = δ²L + (1/2)tr(h)·δL + L₀·[−(1/2)h^{ab}h_{ab} + (1/4)(tr h)²]. The wrong coefficient distorts the kinetic structure, pulling weight from spin-2 into gauge sectors, causing non-zero spin-1 and spin-0w. Fix the coefficient, then validate FP sanity check at Λ→0: Tr(K·P²)≈2.5k², spin-1=0, spin-0w=0.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:52:04Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:52:04Z"}

{"id":"TGR-889","title":"Generalize HypersurfaceProperties to SubmanifoldProperties","description":"Refactor src/gr/hypersurface.jl: (1) Rename HypersurfaceProperties → SubmanifoldProperties with new field codimension::Int (default 1 for backward compat); (2) For codimension k, store k normal vectors and k extrinsic curvatures; (3) Update define_hypersurface! to call define_submanifold!(reg, name; manifold, metric, codimension=1, ...); (4) Keep define_hypersurface! as convenience wrapper; (5) Extend induced_metric_expr for codim-k: γ_{ab} = g_{ab} - Σᵢ σᵢ nᵢ_a nᵢ_b. Backward-compatible: all existing tests must pass unchanged. ~150 lines changed.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:06Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:06Z"}

{"id":"TGR-jpo","title":"Implement analytic FP cross-check from BC params (Approach 3)","description":"Construct predicted momentum-space form factors algebraically from Bueno-Cano parameters, without using the perturbation engine. Use known standard kernel traces: FP Tr(K·P²)=(5/2)k², R² Tr(K·P⁰ˢ)=3k⁴, Ric² Tr(K·P²)=(5/4)k⁴ etc. Build function bc_to_form_factors(bc::BuenoCanoParams, k², Λ) → (f_spin2, f_spin0s). Compare against Approach 1 results at 5-10 test points across k²∈[0.5,4.0], Λ∈[0,0.5]. Verify ±1% agreement. This is an independent algebraic check that does not depend on the perturbation engine.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:52:23Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:52:23Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Session 27 findings: pre-built FP kernel passes all checks perfectly. Bug is in perturbation engine → Fourier → simplify path: canonicalizer fails to combine equivalent tensor structures with mixed index positions (e.g. h[d1,-d1] vs h[-d1,d1] are both tr(h) but not recognized as same). This produces 8 Fourier terms instead of 4 for flat-space δ²R, yielding spin-2 off by 2.5x. Also: TGR-l8h √g coeff fix (1/8→1/4) appears wrong — 1/8 is correct for Taylor-coefficient convention. Root cause: canonicalize + collect_terms in simplify pipeline doesn't normalize trace expressions with symmetric tensors at mixed index positions in momentum space.","status":"open"}

{"id":"TGR-dp3","title":"Canonicalizer fails to combine symmetric tensor traces at mixed index positions","description":"In Fourier-transformed perturbation expressions, h[d1,-d1] and h[-d1,d1] (both tr(h)) are not recognized as identical by canonicalize+collect_terms. This causes δ²R to produce 8 Fourier terms instead of 4 (FP kernel), with spin-2 projection off by factor 5/2. Reproducer: compute δricci_scalar(mp,2) on flat background, Fourier transform, spin_project — gives 10.625 instead of 4.25 for spin-2. Pre-built build_FP_momentum_kernel works perfectly, confirming projectors are correct. Blocker for TGR-76k.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-13T15:48:55Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-13T15:48:55Z"}

new_value: Fixed: reverted sqrt_g coefficient from 1//4 to 1//8 in example 15

{"description":"CORRECTED DIAGNOSIS (session 28): The crosscheck failure is NOT in collect_terms/canonicalize. spin_project correctly handles shared dummies and mixed index positions (verified with FP kernel). The real bug: δ²R from the perturbation engine has A-type coefficient 5/4 (h^{ab}h_{ab}k²) instead of expected 1/2, giving spin-2=6.25 instead of 2.5. Likely a convention mismatch between expand_perturbation and the crosscheck formula, or a bug in the second-order inverse metric expansion. See HANDOFF-next-session.md for deep research plan.","title":"δ²R perturbation engine gives wrong spin-2 coefficient (misdiagnosed as canonicalizer bug)"}

new_value: {"notes":"Session 30: Root cause fully identified. Two interacting bugs: (1) to_fourier mishandles ∂(product) for quadratic forms — treats as k×product instead of distributing via Leibniz with correct momentum routing; (2) contract_metrics can't contract metrics with TDeriv partners. δ²Ric has 2 unexpanded ∂(h×∂h) terms giving spurious spin-2=2.5 and 18 terms with uncontracted g's giving 1.25. Total 3.75 vs correct 0. Simplest fix: Option D — detect and drop total-divergence terms before Fourier transform. See HANDOFF-next-session.md for full analysis."}

old_value: {"id":"TGR-7fj","title":"Document and test -2 sign convention for R²/Ric² kernel contributions","description":"The full kinetic kernel for S = ∫√g(κR + α₁R² + α₂Ric²) on flat background is K = κ·K_EH - 2α₁·K_{R²} - 2α₂·K_{Ric²}. The -2 factor was verified against Buoninfante form factors (2012.11829 Eq.2.13). The perturbation engine pipeline uses: K_EH = extract_kernel_v2(h^{ab}δ¹G_{ab}), K_R² = extract_kernel_v2((δ¹R)²), K_Ric² = extract_kernel_v2((δ¹Ric)²). The -2 sign is NOT a bug in the extraction; it comes from the relationship between the action and the kinetic operator. Must add tests verifying all 4 combinations (κ only, +R², +Ric², both) against analytic form factors.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T09:39:28Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T09:39:28Z"}

{"id":"TGR-om8","title":"HANDOFF documents wrong formula for EH bilinear: use h^{ab}δ¹G_{ab} not δ²R+cross","description":"The HANDOFFs from sessions 30-31 use the formula δ²(√gR) = δ²R + (1/2)tr(h)δ¹R. While algebraically correct, this decomposition is WRONG for kernel extraction because the cross term (1/2)tr(h)δ¹R has n_L=0,n_R=2 and its spin projection gives (0, 1.5) not the expected (1.25, -0.5). The CORRECT approach for the EH bilinear form is h^{ab}δ¹G_{ab} = h^{ab}(δ¹R_{ab} - (1/2)g_{ab}δ¹R), which gives the FP kernel directly. Verified: spin-2=2.5, spin-0s=-1.0.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T09:39:18Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T09:39:18Z"}

new_value: Sign convention documented in HANDOFF and tested in test_kernel_extraction.jl (5 parameter combinations)
HANDOFF-next-session.md rewritten with correct formulas and pipeline

new_value: {"notes":"Session 32 progress: flat-space crosscheck COMPLETE via extract_kernel_direct (h^{ab}d1G_{ab} matches FP to machine precision). dS crosscheck: gauge sectors partly fixed (spin-0w=0 at all Lambda) but spin-1 has residual proportional to Lambda. Root cause: the bilinear h^{ab}(d1G_{ab}+Lambda*h_{ab}) needs additional background curvature corrections from the contracted Bianchi identity on MSS. The old pipeline (Q=d2R+sqrt_g_correction) may be needed for dS, adapted to use extract_kernel_direct. Key finding: the -Lambda*h_{ab}h^{ab}+(1/2)Lambda*h^2 cosmological correction fixes spin-0w but not spin-1."}

{"id":"TGR-68n","title":"spin_project has index collision on dS kernels — free indices leak into scalar result","description":"When projecting kernels extracted from MSS (de Sitter) expressions, spin_project produces terms with free indices (e.g. k^o k^o, g^{oo}) instead of a fully scalar result. Root cause: the projector's internal dummy indices (from theta/omega) collide with coefficient indices after ensure_no_dummy_clash. At Λ=0 (flat limit) this doesn't manifest because Λ-dependent terms vanish. At finite Λ, the collision creates uncontractable free index pairs. Diagnosis: inspecting spin_project(K_EH_dS, :spin2) shows 3 of 7 terms have 2 free Up indices named 'o'. Fix needed: ensure all dummy indices in the projector are unique w.r.t. the full coefficient expression.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T17:20:28Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T17:20:28Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"The residual g^{oo} issue is handled by _eval_spin_scalar fallback contraction. The REAL blocker for dS crosscheck is that flat Barnes-Rivers projectors give wrong results on dS kernels — the projectors need MSS adaptation (background curvature modifies the eigenvalue spectrum). This is a theoretical extension (dS-adapted spin projectors), not a bug in the existing code."}

old_value: {"id":"TGR-nzk","title":"Implement integrate_geodesic() in DiffEq extension","description":"In ext/TensorGRDiffEqExt.jl, implement integrate_geodesic(geod::GeodesicEquation, x0::Vector, v0::Vector, τ_span::Tuple; solver=Tsit5(), kwargs...) that: (1) builds ODEProblem from geodesic_rhs!, (2) solves with DifferentialEquations.solve(), (3) returns solution with fields .t (proper time), .x (positions), .v (velocities). old_value: {"id":"TGR-zkw","title":"Auto-register Killing equation as rewrite rules","description":"Extend define_killing\\!(reg, :ξ; manifold, metric) to automatically register the Killing equation £_ξ g_{ab} = ∇_a ξ_b + ∇_b ξ_a = 0 as a RewriteRule. Currently define_killing\\! only stores metadata but doesn't enforce the equation. Use make_rule() with the Killing equation LHS → 0. Also add check_killing(reg, :ξ, expr) that simplifies an expression using Killing rules. Located in src/gr/killing.jl. ~80 lines.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:52:40Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:52:40Z"}

old_value: {"id":"TGR-3gx","title":"Implement order-2 curvature syzygies as rewrite rules","description":"Add syzygies to src/gr/invariants.jl: (1) Gauss-Bonnet identity in 4D: E₄ = R² - 4Ric² + Kretschmann is topological → Weyl² = Kretschmann - 2Ric² + R²/3; (2) In 3D: Weyl=0 → Kretschmann = 2Ric² - R²/2; (3) register_invariant_syzygies!(reg; dim) that adds these as RewriteRules; (4) simplify_invariants(expr; reg, dim) that applies syzygies to reduce to independent basis. Depends on dim from manifold registry. ~150 lines.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:48Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:48Z"}

{"id":"TGR-0mg","title":"Implement Gauss-Codazzi relations as rewrite rules","description":"Add src/gr/gauss_codazzi.jl with: (1) gauss_equation(reg, hyp) returning RewriteRule: projected ambient Riemann → intrinsic Riemann + extrinsic curvature terms: R^(γ)_{ijkl} = P^a_i P^b_j P^c_k P^d_l R_{abcd} + K_{ik}K_{jl} - K_{il}K_{jk}; (2) codazzi_equation(reg, hyp) returning ∇_i K_{jk} - ∇_j K_{ik} = P^a_i P^b_j P^c_k n^d R_{abcd}; (3) Register as rules via register_rule!. These relate ambient and intrinsic curvature on embedded hypersurfaces. ~200 lines.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:19Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:19Z"}

{"id":"TGR-vhp","title":"Implement equation of state types for matter","description":"Add to src/gr/matter.jl: (1) abstract type EquationOfState; (2) PolytropicEOS(K, Γ) where p=Kρ^Γ; (3) LinearEOS(c_s²) where p=c_s²·ρ; (4) TabularEOS(ρ_table, p_table) with interpolation; (5) eos_pressure(eos, ρ) and eos_sound_speed(eos, ρ) functions. These are used by the TOV solver to close the hydrostatic equilibrium system. ~100 lines.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:04Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:04Z"}

{"id":"TGR-f0c","title":"Implement axial symmetry metric ansatz generator","description":"Add metric_ansatz(reg, manifold, ::AxialSymmetry; coords=[:t,:r,:θ,:φ]) returning the most general stationary axisymmetric metric with 4 free functions of (r,θ): ds²=-N²dt²+g_rr dr²+g_θθ dθ²+g_φφ(dφ-ωdt)² (Lewis-Papapetrou form). Cross-term g_{tφ}=-g_φφ·ω encodes frame dragging. Return (metric, free_functions=[N,g_rr,g_θθ,g_φφ,ω]). Test: verify Kerr metric is special case with known N,ω,g_rr,g_θθ,g_φφ. ~200 lines.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:52:39Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:52:39Z"}

{"id":"TGR-760","title":"Implement GHY boundary term extraction","description":"Add to src/gr/hypersurface.jl: (1) ghy_boundary_term(reg, hypersurface) returning ∫_∂M √h K where h=induced metric determinant, K=trace of extrinsic curvature; (2) As TensorExpr: 2*K where K = g^{ab}K_{ab}; (3) Add ibp_with_boundary(expr, field; registry) variant of ibp() that returns (bulk_term, boundary_term) instead of discarding boundary. Modify src/algebra/ibp.jl to support this. The GHY term is essential for well-posed variational problems with boundaries. Test: verify δ(S_EH + S_GHY)/δg = 0 gives correct Einstein equations. ~200 lines.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:12Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:12Z"}

{"id":"TGR-141","title":"Add order-3 cubic curvature invariants to catalog","description":"Extend src/gr/invariants.jl with the 6 independent cubic invariants (already computed in benchmarks/bench_12_6deriv_dS.jl): I₁=R³, I₂=R·Ric², I₃=Ric³ (trace), I₄=R·Riem², I₅=Ric·Riem² (contraction), I₆=Riem³ (cycle). For each: (1) Add InvariantEntry with expression builder using registered curvature tensors; (2) Add dimension constraints (some vanish in 3D); (3) Add Bueno-Cano coefficient metadata from BC_CUBIC_COEFFICIENTS. Port the build_I1..build_I6 functions from bench_12 into the invariant catalog. ~200 lines.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:54Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:54Z"}

{"id":"TGR-r4i","title":"Implement TOV equation ODE system","description":"Create src/solvers/tov.jl with: (1) TOVSystem struct storing EOS and central density ρ_c; (2) tov_rhs!(du, u, p, r) implementing the TOV equations: dm/dr = 4πr²ρ, dp/dr = -(ρ+p)(m+4πr³p)/(r(r-2m)), with state u=[m,p]; (3) setup_tov(eos, ρ_c) returning TOVSystem with boundary conditions m(0)=0, p(0)=p(ρ_c); (4) Handle the r=0 regularity condition via series expansion. Include in TensorGR.jl. ~200 lines.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:06Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:06Z"}

{"id":"TGR-prb","title":"Implement Israel junction conditions for thin shells","description":"Add src/gr/junction.jl with: (1) JunctionProperties struct storing two ambient regions (inside/outside), shared hypersurface, and surface stress-energy S_{ab}; (2) define_junction!(reg, hyp; inside_reg, outside_reg) registering the setup; (3) israel_equation(reg, junction) returning [K_{ab}]⁺₋ - γ_{ab}[K]⁺₋ = -8π S_{ab} where [·]⁺₋ denotes jump across surface; (4) solve_junction(reg, junction, S_ab) solving for metric discontinuity. Used for domain walls, thin shells, brane-world scenarios. ~250 lines.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:25Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:25Z"}

old_value: {"id":"TGR-avk","title":"Constraints: Implement automatic trace-free enforcement in simplify pipeline","description":"Implement automatic enforcement of trace-free constraints: when a tensor T is registered as trace-free (T^a_a = 0), any expression containing a trace of T should automatically simplify to zero. Extend TensorProperties with is_tracefree::Bool field. Modify the simplify pipeline (specifically contract_metrics or a new trace_enforce step) to detect and eliminate traces of trace-free tensors. Also handle partial traces: if T^{ab}_{cd} is trace-free on (a,c), then T^{ab}_{ad} = 0. Function: set_tracefree!(reg, :T; pairs=[(1,3)]) to specify which index pairs are trace-free. ~80 LOC modifying src/registry.jl + src/algebra/contraction.jl. Reference: xAct-contrib/TraceFree package documentation; Wald, General Relativity (1984), Sec 3.2. Test: Given trace-free Weyl C_{abcd}, simplify(g^{ac} C_{abcd}) returns TScalar(0), matching the Weyl trace-free identity.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:34:03Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:34:03Z"}

{"id":"TGR-ihp.1","title":"Implement AllContractions(expr, metric)","description":"Generate all possible full contractions of a tensor expression using a given metric. For a rank-2n tensor, enumerate all ways to pair 2n indices into n pairs, contract each pair with metric g^{ab}. Number of pairings = (2n-1)!! = (2n)!/(2^n n!). For example, rank-4 tensor T_{abcd}: contractions are g^{ab}g^{cd}T_{abcd}, g^{ac}g^{bd}T_{abcd}, g^{ad}g^{bc}T_{abcd} (3 = 3!! pairings). Must handle symmetries of the input tensor to avoid generating equivalent contractions. API: all_contractions(expr, metric_name; registry=reg) -\u003e Vector{TensorExpr}. New file: src/algebra/all_contractions.jl. Ground truth: Nutma (2014) Sec 2.1. ~100 LOC.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:11:12Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:11:12Z"}

{"id":"TGR-9tx.1","title":"Harmonics: Implement ScalarHarmonic type Y_{lm}","description":"Implement ScalarHarmonic type representing Y_{lm}(theta, phi) with quantum numbers (l, m). Properties: l \u003e= 0, |m| \u003c= l, eigenvalue of Laplacian on S^2. New file: src/harmonics/scalar_harmonics.jl. Type should store (l::Int, m::Int) and integrate with TensorExpr AST (probably as TScalar wrapper or new leaf node). Must support conjugation Y*_{lm} = (-1)^m Y_{l,-m}. Ground truth: Martel \u0026 Poisson, Phys. Rev. D 71, 104003 (2005), Sec II.A. Test: construct Y_{2,1}, verify quantum number validation rejects l\u003c0 or |m|\u003el. ~100 LOC.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:09:38Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:09:38Z"}

{"id":"TGR-ulo.3","title":"Implement SortCovDsToBox enhancement","description":"Enhance covariant derivative sorting to convert to Box operator form more aggressively than current. Current box() in src/gr/box.jl creates Box = g^{ab}nabla_a nabla_b, but sort_covds does not attempt to produce this form. Enhancement: (1) detect g^{ab}nabla_a nabla_b patterns after commuting derivatives, (2) replace with Box operator, (3) handle multiple Box applications (Box^2, etc.), (4) recognize grad_squared patterns nabla_a phi nabla^a phi. API: sort_covds_to_box(expr, covd_name; registry=reg) -\u003e TensorExpr. Ground truth: Nutma Sec 4.3. ~100 LOC in src/gr/sort_covds.jl.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:12:23Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:12:23Z"}

{"id":"TGR-6lj","title":"Constraints: Implement automatic divergence-free enforcement in simplify pipeline","description":"Implement automatic enforcement of divergence-free constraints: when a tensor T is registered as divergence-free (nabla_a T^{ab...} = 0), any covariant divergence of T should automatically simplify to zero. Extend TensorProperties with is_divfree::Bool and divfree_index::Int (which index is divergence-free). Modify the simplify pipeline to detect nabla_a T^{a...} patterns after CovD expansion and set them to zero. Must work with both partial derivatives and covariant derivatives. Function: set_divfree!(reg, :T; covd=:D, index=1). ~80 LOC modifying src/registry.jl + src/algebra/simplify.jl. Reference: xAct-contrib/DivFree package documentation; Einstein tensor nabla_a G^{ab} = 0, stress-energy conservation nabla_a T^{ab} = 0. Test: Given divergence-free T^{ab}, simplify(TDeriv(down(:a), T^{ab}, :D)) returns TScalar(0), matching conservation equation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:34:07Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:34:07Z"}

{"id":"TGR-ihp.2","title":"Implement MakeContractionAnsatz","description":"Build the most general scalar ansatz from all independent contractions of given tensor products. Given tensors T1, T2, ..., form all possible products and contract all indices with metrics. Each independent contraction gets an undetermined coefficient c_i. API: contraction_ansatz(tensors, metric_name; registry=reg) -\u003e TSum of c_i * contraction_i. Should reuse all_contractions() internally. Example: contraction_ansatz([:Riem, :Riem], :g) returns c1*RabcdRabcd + c2*RabRab + c3*R^2 (modulo symmetry). Ground truth: Nutma Sec 2.2. ~100 LOC in src/algebra/all_contractions.jl.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:11:16Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:11:16Z"}

{"id":"TGR-9tx.2","title":"Harmonics: Implement scalar harmonic algebra (Clebsch-Gordan)","description":"Implement product algebra for scalar harmonics: Y_{l1,m1} * Y_{l2,m2} = sum over (l3,m3) of Clebsch-Gordan coefficients times Y_{l3,m3}. Selection rules: |l1-l2| \u003c= l3 \u003c= l1+l2, m3 = m1+m2. Implement Clebsch-Gordan coefficient computation (or Wigner 3j symbols). File: src/harmonics/clebsch_gordan.jl. Ground truth: standard angular momentum coupling theory. Test: Y_{1,0} * Y_{1,0} decomposes into Y_{0,0} and Y_{2,0} with correct coefficients. ~100 LOC.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:09:48Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:09:48Z"}

{"id":"TGR-ulo.2","title":"Implement SortCovDsToDiv enhancement","description":"Enhance the current stub sort_covds_to_div (in src/gr/sort_covds.jl, currently returns input unchanged) to actually convert expressions to divergence form nabla_a V^a. Algorithm: (1) identify terms with covariant derivatives, (2) use integration by parts (existing ibp.jl) to move derivatives onto fewer factors, (3) recognize divergence patterns nabla_a(...^a...), (4) collect divergence terms. This is useful for conservation laws and boundary term identification. API: sort_covds_to_div(expr, covd_name; registry=reg) -\u003e TensorExpr. Ground truth: Nutma Sec 4.2. ~100 LOC in src/gr/sort_covds.jl.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:12:19Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:12:19Z"}

{"id":"TGR-wd6","title":"Constraints Validation: Weyl tensor trace-free and Bianchi identities auto-simplify","description":"End-to-end validation of the constraint enforcement system using the Weyl tensor. Setup: register Weyl C_{abcd} as (1) trace-free on all index pairs: C^a_{bad} = 0, (2) first Bianchi identity: C_{a[bcd]} = 0, (3) divergence-free in vacuum: nabla^a C_{abcd} = 0. Verify: (a) g^{ac} C_{abcd} automatically simplifies to zero (trace-free), (b) C_{abcd} + C_{acdb} + C_{adbc} automatically simplifies to zero (cyclic/Bianchi), (c) in vacuum spacetime, nabla^a C_{abcd} simplifies to zero (divergence-free). Ground truth: Wald, General Relativity (1984), Eq 3.2.28, 3.2.14; standard Weyl tensor identities. Test: STRING MATCH — all three expressions simplify to TScalar(0), confirming automatic constraint enforcement.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:34:12Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:34:12Z"}

{"id":"TGR-ihp.3","title":"Implement contraction filtering by symmetry","description":"Use tensor symmetries (Young tableaux already in src/algebra/young.jl) to eliminate redundant contractions from all_contractions(). Two contractions are equivalent if they differ only by relabeling of contracted (dummy) indices and the tensor symmetries make the expressions equal. Algorithm: (1) canonicalize each contraction, (2) collect unique canonical forms, (3) return independent set. Should integrate with existing canonicalize() and Young tableau infrastructure. Ground truth: Nutma Sec 2.3, Fulling et al (1992). ~100 LOC.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:11:20Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:11:20Z"}

{"id":"TGR-bqf.1","title":"Harmonics: Implement even-parity vector harmonics Y^a_{lm}","description":"Implement even-parity (polar) vector spherical harmonics: Y^a_{lm} = D^a Y_{lm}, where D^a is the covariant derivative on S^2. These are the gradient-type vector harmonics. New type EvenVectorHarmonic with (l, m) quantum numbers, l \u003e= 1 (l=0 gives zero). Must carry a single S^2 index (angular). Properties: D_a Y^a_{lm} = -l(l+1) Y_{lm} (divergence). File: src/harmonics/vector_harmonics.jl. Ground truth: Martel \u0026 Poisson Eq 2.5. Test: verify divergence relation. ~100 LOC.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:10:11Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:10:11Z"}

{"id":"TGR-99d.1","title":"Implement SymmetrizeCovDs","description":"Force covariant derivatives into symmetrized form: nabla_{(a} nabla_{b)} = (1/2)(nabla_a nabla_b + nabla_b nabla_a). When acting on a scalar, nabla_{(a} nabla_{b)} phi = nabla_a nabla_b phi (already symmetric). When acting on tensors, use commutator relation [nabla_a, nabla_b]V^c = R^c_{dab}V^d to write nabla_a nabla_b = nabla_{(a} nabla_{b)} + (1/2)R... This is the inverse operation of commute_covds: instead of sorting to canonical order, express in manifestly symmetric form. API: symmetrize_covds(expr, covd_name; registry=reg) -\u003e TensorExpr. Ground truth: Nutma Sec 5. ~100 LOC in src/gr/sort_covds.jl.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:12:57Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:12:57Z"}

{"id":"TGR-8nx.1","title":"Implement algebra-valued differential forms","description":"Extend existing exterior calculus (src/exterior/forms.jl) to support forms valued in a Lie algebra. A Lie-algebra-valued p-form is omega = omega^I_{a1...ap} T_I dx^{a1} wedge ... wedge dx^{ap} where T_I are Lie algebra generators. Implement AlgValuedForm type wrapping (form_degree, lie_algebra_bundle, tensor_expr). Must support: wedge product with Lie bracket [omega wedge eta]^I = f^I_{JK} omega^J wedge eta^K, exterior derivative d acting on form indices only. Connection 1-form: A = A^I_a T_I dx^a. New file: src/exterior/algebra_forms.jl. Ground truth: Nakahara Ch 11, Sec 11.1. ~100 LOC.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:15:26Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:15:26Z"}

{"id":"TGR-9tx.3","title":"Harmonics: Implement angular Laplacian eigenvalue relation","description":"Implement the angular Laplacian acting on scalar harmonics: Delta_Omega Y_{lm} = -l(l+1) Y_{lm}. This is a simplification rule that replaces Laplacian(Y_{lm}) with the eigenvalue times Y_{lm}. Should integrate with the rules engine (RewriteRule). The Laplacian on S^2 is Delta_Omega = (1/sin theta) d/d_theta (sin theta d/d_theta) + (1/sin^2 theta) d^2/d_phi^2. Ground truth: Martel \u0026 Poisson Eq 2.1. File: src/harmonics/laplacian.jl. Test: simplify(laplacian_S2(Y_{3,1})) == -12 * Y_{3,1}. ~80 LOC.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:09:55Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:09:55Z"}

{"id":"TGR-bqf.3","title":"Harmonics: Implement vector harmonic orthogonality relations","description":"Implement orthogonality integral relations for vector harmonics on S^2. Key relations: integral(Y^a_{lm} Y^*_{a,l'm'}) = l(l+1) delta_{ll'} delta_{mm'}, integral(X^a_{lm} X^*_{a,l'm'}) = l(l+1) delta_{ll'} delta_{mm'}, integral(Y^a_{lm} X^*_{a,l'm'}) = 0 (even-odd orthogonal). These become simplification rules for products integrated over S^2. File: src/harmonics/orthogonality.jl. Ground truth: Martel \u0026 Poisson Sec II.C. Test: verify all three orthogonality relations as RewriteRules. ~80 LOC.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:10:22Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:10:22Z"}

{"id":"TGR-jc7.1","title":"Harmonics: Implement even-parity tensor harmonics Y^{ab}_{lm}, Z^{ab}_{lm}","description":"Implement even-parity (polar) symmetric tensor spherical harmonics on S^2. Two types: (1) Y^{ab}_{lm} = Omega^{ab} Y_{lm} where Omega^{ab} is the unit round metric on S^2, and (2) Z^{ab}_{lm} = (D^a D^b + (1/2)l(l+1) Omega^{ab}) Y_{lm} (symmetric trace-free, STF). Quantum numbers: l \u003e= 0 for Y^{ab}, l \u003e= 2 for Z^{ab} (l=0,1 give zero for Z). File: src/harmonics/tensor_harmonics.jl. Ground truth: Martel \u0026 Poisson Eqs 2.14-2.17. Test: verify trace(Z^{ab}_{lm}) = 0. ~120 LOC.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:10:35Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:10:35Z"}

{"id":"TGR-8nx.2","title":"Implement gauge-covariant exterior derivative","description":"Implement gauge-covariant exterior derivative D = d + A wedge (.) acting on algebra-valued forms. For a Lie-algebra-valued p-form omega: D omega = d omega + [A, omega] = d omega^I T_I + f^I_{JK} A^J wedge omega^K T_I. Properties: D^2 omega = [F, omega] (curvature as obstruction to D^2=0). Must handle: (1) D on scalar-valued forms (just d), (2) D on adjoint-valued forms (with structure constants), (3) Leibniz rule for wedge products of mixed-valued forms. Extends src/exterior/algebra_forms.jl. Ground truth: Nakahara Sec 11.1; Eguchi, Gilkey \u0026 Hanson, Phys. Rep. 66, 213 (1980). ~100 LOC.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:15:38Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:15:38Z"}

{"id":"TGR-ihp.4","title":"Validation: AllContractions of R_{abcd}R_{efgh}","description":"Validate AllContractions by computing all independent quadratic Riemann invariants. all_contractions(Riem*Riem, :g) should produce exactly 7 independent full contractions before DDI reduction: (1) R_{abcd}R^{abcd} (Kretschner), (2) R_{abcd}R^{acbd}, (3) R_{ab}R^{ab}, (4) R^2, (5) R_{abcd}R^{ab}g^{cd}-type, (6-7) further metric contractions.
